openFindings.title: Cleanup script bypasses governance_audit on bulk tier downgrades and deletes existing audit rows
openFindings.evidence: Bulk UPDATE memory_index SET importance_tier='important' at :324-330 emits zero governance_audit rows. Generic reference-cleanup loop at :309 deletes governance_audit rows for deleted memory ids. Both actions contradict Wave-1 audit-emit contract in the highest-volume operator scenario. Does not violate Invariant 3 (moving AWAY from constitutional), but makes governance_audit non-authoritative for compliance/forensics after --apply.

openFindings.title: Redundant tier-downgrade governance_audit emitters across 3 code sites
openFindings.evidence: Three code sites emit action='tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path'. Iter-3 control-flow trace confirms max redundancy is 2 rows per decision (S2 and S5 in iteration-003.md), not 3. Handler narrow condition + SQL-layer unconditional emit can co-fire in one scenario; post-insert-metadata cannot co-fire with the handler. P2 severity confirmed.

openFindings.title: Wave-1 strategy description drift: importance_tier still in ALLOWED_POST_INSERT_COLUMNS
openFindings.evidence: strategy.md claims the allowlist removal as Wave-1 P1-018 fix; in practice the allowlist still contains 'importance_tier' (post-insert-metadata.ts:56) but the guard was hoisted above the SET builder. Functional equivalence holds; documentation drift only.

openFindings.title: Constitutional→critical downgrade emits no governance_audit
openFindings.evidence: Guard only triggers for importanceTier==='constitutional' requests. A request importanceTier='critical' against a currently-constitutional row on a non-constitutional path downgrades to 'critical' without any typed governance_audit. Handler audit condition nextTier==='important' also fails. Observability gap, not Invariant-3 violation.

openFindings.title: Governance-audit emission pattern duplicated across 5 sites without shared helper or action-string constants
openFindings.evidence: tryRecordTierDowngradeAudit helper is module-local to vector-index-mutations.ts (not exported). 4 of 5 sites duplicate recordGovernanceAudit payload inline with subtle drift (anchor sentinel, metadata shape). Action string 'tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path' appears at 5 production + 3 test sites as bare literal with no enum/constant. Action string 'checkpoint_restore_excluded_path_rejected' appears at 1 production + 1 test bare literal. Recommend: move tryRecordTierDowngradeAudit into scope-governance.ts and export; define GOVERNANCE_AUDIT_ACTIONS const-namespace enum. checkpoints.ts deferred-flush variant remains legitimate.
